description:
## Goal

Introduce the position-tracking primitives. No AST/IR change yet; this lays the foundation that .20.2 and later sit on top of.

## Why byte offsets, not line/col

Today `Token { tok, line: u32, col: u32 }` (`src/lexer.rs:71-76`) carries 1-indexed line/col. That's fine for "error at L:C" string output, but it breaks down for snippet rendering:

- **Multi-byte UTF-8.** `self.col` increments per *byte* (`lexer.rs:110`), so any non-ASCII character makes columns lie. fz source today is ASCII-clean but the language won't be ASCII-forever.
- **Tab expansion.** Cannot align carets if columns assume `\t` is one column.
- **End positions.** A token's *end* line/col requires re-scanning. A byte range `[start, end)` is one comparison.

Byte offsets fix all three: the renderer (.20.6) computes display line/col from the source bytes when it draws a snippet, and gets to handle tabs / unicode / line-wrapping correctly in one place.

## Deliverables

### 1. `src/diag/span.rs` (new module, mounted as `mod diag;` in `main.rs`)

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub struct FileId(pub u32);

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub struct Span {
    pub file: FileId,
    pub start: u32,   // byte offset into file bytes
    pub end: u32,     // exclusive
}

impl Span {
    pub const DUMMY: Span = Span { file: FileId(u32::MAX), start: 0, end: 0 };
    pub fn is_dummy(self) -> bool { self.file.0 == u32::MAX }
    pub fn merge(self, other: Span) -> Span { /* file must match; min/max */ }
    pub fn len(self) -> u32 { self.end - self.start }
}
```

`DUMMY` exists for synthesized AST nodes that have no source location *yet* — macro expansion (.20.3) will replace these with proper `SpanOrigin` lineage. The renderer must handle DUMMY gracefully (no source pointer, just "(generated)").

### 2. `src/diag/source_map.rs`

```rust
pub struct SourceFile {
    pub name: String,        // path or synthetic label like "<prelude>"
    pub bytes: Arc<str>,     // shared with parser/renderer
    line_starts: OnceCell<Vec<u32>>,  // lazy line-offset index
}

pub struct SourceMap {
    files: Vec<SourceFile>,
}

impl SourceMap {
    pub fn add_file(&mut self, name: String, bytes: Arc<str>) -> FileId;
    pub fn file(&self, id: FileId) -> &SourceFile;
    /// Returns (line_1based, col_1based, line_bytes_slice) for a byte offset.
    /// Line/col compute on demand; line_starts is built once on first call.
    pub fn locate(&self, span: Span) -> Location;
}

pub struct Location {
    pub file: FileId,
    pub line: u32,    // 1-based for display
    pub col: u32,     // 1-based, **display column** (tab-expanded, unicode-width-aware in v2; byte-count in v1)
    pub line_bytes: Range<u32>,   // start..end of the line containing `span.start`
}
```

`Arc<str>` rationale: lexer holds `&str` slices into the bytes; spans index into them. The bytes need to outlive the lexer (renderer needs them at the end of compilation). `Arc<str>` is the cheap way to share without lifetime gymnastics. `Rc<str>` would work too — pick `Arc` so a future multi-threaded driver isn't blocked.

`line_starts` caching: line-offset computation is O(n) per file; the renderer hits it once per emitted diagnostic. Cache it as `OnceCell<Vec<u32>>` populated on first lookup. The 4-byte-per-line cost is negligible vs. correctness/clarity.

### 3. Lexer changes (`src/lexer.rs`)

- `Lexer` adds `start_offset: u32` (the file's base byte offset, 0 for a fresh lex; non-zero when the test runner concatenates prelude — see Multi-source below).
- `Token` becomes `{ tok, span: Span }`. Drop `line, col` fields.
- `Lexer::new(src: &str, file: FileId) -> Self`. Each `bump()` tracks `self.pos` (already done at `lexer.rs:107-112`) which IS the byte offset. Emit `Span { file, start: tok_start_pos, end: self.pos }` per token.
- `LexError { msg: String, span: Span }`. `Display` prints `lex error: <msg>` (the location is the renderer's job from .20.6 onwards). For .20.1, the temporary `Display` impl can still write `lex error at offset {span.start}: {msg}` to keep the harness output usable until .20.7 lands.
- The current `line: u32, col: u32` fields on `Lexer` go away; no consumer needs them once spans are the surface.

### 4. Test-runner prelude concatenation (`src/test_runner.rs:58`)

Today `format!("{}\n{}", PRELUDE, user_src)` is a single source. After .20.1:

- Register `<prelude>` as `FileId(0)` and the user file as `FileId(1)` *separately*. Run the lexer twice (once per file), concatenate the token streams, parser consumes the combined stream as before. Each token already carries the right `FileId` in its span.
- The "PRELUDE corrupts line numbers" bug described in the .20 epic is fixed as a side effect.

### 5. Tests

- Round-trip: lex a small snippet, verify span ranges point at the right bytes (`&src[span.start..span.end]` matches the lexeme).
- Multi-line: lex a 3-line snippet, verify line offsets in `SourceMap::locate` are correct.
- Tab handling: lex `"\tfoo"` with display column expansion at tab=4 → col should be 5 not 2. (v1 may stub this at byte-count and mark TODO for .20.6; do whichever — but make the choice explicit and consistent.)
- Multi-file: register two files, verify that `Span` distinguishes them correctly.

## Non-goals

- AST changes (.20.2).
- Renderer (.20.6) — `Location` / `SourceFile` provide enough for the renderer to do its work later; this ticket stops at "the data structure is right".

## Notes for the implementer

- Cargo.toml stays untouched. No external crate.
- The `diag` module is the home for everything in the .20 arc — `span.rs`, `source_map.rs`, and (in later tickets) `diagnostic.rs`, `render.rs`.
- Don't bother making `Span` field-encoded for tininess (e.g. file_id packed into the start word). 12 bytes Copy is fine; we don't churn them.
title: Span + SourceMap foundation; lexer carries byte offsets
